description: Length-matched V2 facts for 3-body conservation laws domain.

facts.context: Total energy of a 3-body gravitational system equals:
facts.truth: kinetic energy (with 1/2 factor) minus gravitational potential energy
distractors: kinetic energy without the one-half factor minus potential energy terms
kinetic energy plus potential energy (wrong sign on gravity attraction)
velocity (not velocity squared) used in the kinetic energy expression

facts.context: Total linear momentum of a 3-body system equals:
facts.truth: the vector sum of m_i times v_i for all three bodies
distractors: only the first body's momentum while ignoring the other two
total system mass times center-of-mass velocity (wrong formula)
sum of kinetic energies rather than momentum vector components

facts.context: Total angular momentum of a 3-body system equals:
facts.truth: the sum of m_i times (r_i cross v_i) over all bodies
distractors: cross products computed without the required mass factors m_i
dot product of r and v instead of the correct cross product
angular momentum from only one body ignoring the other two

facts.context: The Jacobi constant in the circular restricted 3-body problem equals:
facts.truth: centrifugal term plus twice the effective potential minus velocity squared
distractors: centrifugal term plus the potential without the required factor of two
linear mean motion n instead of n-squared in the centrifugal expression
velocity squared added rather than subtracted (wrong sign convention)

facts.context: Orbital speed at radius r in a Kepler orbit with semi-major axis a satisfies:
facts.truth: v-squared equals GM times the quantity (2/r minus 1/a) exactly
distractors: the factor of 2 on the 1/r term is missing from the expression
v not v-squared appears on the left side giving wrong dimensions
gravitational parameter GM is missing the central body mass M

facts.context: Specific orbital energy in the Kepler problem equals:
facts.truth: v-squared over 2 minus GM over r (kinetic minus potential per mass)
distractors: v not v-squared in kinetic term giving incorrect dimensional formula
plus sign between terms instead of minus (wrong potential convention)
missing the factor of 2 in the denominator of the kinetic energy term

facts.context: Specific angular momentum in a Kepler orbit equals:
facts.truth: r times v times sine of the angle between position and velocity vectors
distractors: r times v without any angular factor (valid only at periapsis passage)
r-squared times v which gives an expression with the wrong dimensions
r times v times cosine instead of sine (perpendicular component wrong)

facts.context: Orbital angular momentum of Kepler orbit with semi-major axis a and eccentricity e:
facts.truth: equals reduced mass mu times sqrt of G*M*a*(1 minus e-squared)
distractors: missing the eccentricity correction factor (1 minus e-squared) entirely
missing the reduced mass mu prefactor in the angular momentum formula
uses (1 minus e) instead of (1 minus e-squared) for eccentricity term

facts.context: Jacobi constant velocity relation in the CRTBP states:
facts.truth: v-squared equals centrifugal plus twice the potential minus the Jacobi constant
distractors: the potential energy terms are missing the required factor of two multiplier
v not v-squared appears on the left side creating a dimensional inconsistency
mean motion n not n-squared appears in the centrifugal contribution to energy

facts.context: Energy relation for test particle in the circular restricted 3-body rotating frame:
facts.truth: v-squared equals centrifugal plus twice the effective potential Omega minus C_J
distractors: missing the factor of 2 multiplying the effective potential Omega function
linear mean motion n instead of n-squared appears in centrifugal energy term
wrong sign: minus twice Omega instead of plus twice Omega in the expression
